category_title: Elementary student with ASD and limited verbal communication who protests via screaming and falling to the floor
minimal_context: Sylvia is participating in a whole-group calendar activity using a SmartBoard to review the date, days of the week, and weather.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Sylvia, it is time for the group calendar activity. Please come to the SmartBoard and touch the interactive icon for today's weather.

STUDENT: Wonder Pets to the rescue.

TUTOR: Look at the board. Just pick one of the weather words.

STUDENT: (Steps away from the SmartBoard) Wonder Pets to the rescue.

TUTOR: You need to remain in the designated area and follow directions for calendar time.

STUDENT: (Screams, cries, and falls to the floor on her knees)

options.A: Altered the original plans for the calendar activity and incorporated Sylvia's contributions.
options.B: Responded by saying, "Another one already? No way! I'm so proud of you! Good job, keep going."
options.C: Used the SmartBoard with interactive icons and words for the days and weather.
options.D: Patted Sylvia on the shoulder, smiled, and asked her to pick another weather word.
